title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Language Clinic: Bfs Predecessors
summary: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bfs predecessors increment in deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_bfs_shortest_path_3(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-bfs-predecessors-1i3s8g
# Competency: maze-bfs-predecessors.
# Case 8328: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_bfs_predecessors_1i3s8g(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1i3s8g = "1i3s8g-g8s3i1-8328"
    assert evidence_variant_1i3s8g
    evidence_axes_1i3s8g = (
        "grid1i3s8g",
        "topology8328",
        "searchg8s3i1",
        "event1i3s8g8328",
        "access83281i3s8g",
        "recoveryg8s3i18328",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1i3s8g)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Predecessors model · Theory Bfs Predecessors, keep Bfs Predecessors connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Predecessors model · Theory Bfs Predecessors, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Bfs Predecessors precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Predecessors model · Theory Bfs Predecessors, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4410 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-bfs-predecessors-1i3s8g(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_bfs_predecessors_1i3s8g\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges != oracle_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?predecessor_links != discovered - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fifo_order_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
